# VER-QUIC-CRT-0001 - QUIC Connection Runtime High-Density Execution Verification

## Scope

This verification slice covers the endpoint-side shard host and stable routing seam defined by SPEC-QUIC-CRT. It proves that one connection handle always maps to the same shard, that duplicate ownership is rejected, and that a connection's events stay on one owner path under concurrent posting. It does not attempt to prove timer migration or other downstream CRT slices.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0006
- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0020
- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0094
- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0095

## Verification Method

Execution and inspection evidence from focused unit tests.

## Preconditions

- The Incursa.Quic solution builds successfully.
- The CRT requirement traits are discoverable by the test runner.
- The sharded runtime host and its route registry remain internal to the repository.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the requirement-home tests for stable shard routing, duplicate ownership rejection, single-connection shard ownership, and configurable shard count.
- Run the existing CRT inbox tests that prove the one-consumer/many-producer queue pattern used by the shard host.
- Confirm the host remains bounded to the configured shard count and that connection ownership stays on one shard for the full sequence.

## Expected Result

The sharded runtime host keeps a stable handle-to-shard mapping, rejects duplicate ownership, and routes a single connection's events through one owner path even under concurrent posting.
